Empires & Puzzles Teddy Hero Review: The Pureheart Strategy Guide

Teddy brings a unique sleep mechanic to Empires & Puzzles that can turn the tide of battle—or backfire spectacularly. Is this sleepy bear a strategic mastermind or just hibernating on your bench? We wake him up for a full review.
Teddy: The Bear Essential Guide to Napping on the Battlefield
Teddy, The Pureheart: Napping Through The Apocalypse
Teddy is a 5-star Nature (Green) hero from the
Alliance Quest, released in late 2023. As a member of the

, he represents a tankier, more defensive side of the event's roster. His primary gimmick is "Sleep," a status ailment that acts as a crowd-control disable but with a volatile "wake up on damage" clause. While he might look like a cute kid in a bear onesie, his ability to completely shut down an enemy team—even for a moment—can be terrifying. He’s a tactical hero, not a brute force one. If you like playing 4D chess while your opponents are just matching tiles, Teddy might be your guy.

Teddy's design is refreshingly lighthearted compared to the grimdark aesthetic of many recent heroes. He appears to be a young squire or perhaps just an enthusiastic larper, donned in a bear costume that looks equal parts cozy and protective. The art captures a sense of playful aggression—he's ready to fight, but he's probably going to need a juice box afterwards. It’s a great visual representation of his "Pureheart" title.

These were respectable in 2023, but modern "tanky" heroes are sitting at 1300+ Defense and 2200+ Health.

Teddy falls into the "average" category for legacy heroes. He isn't made of paper, but he won't survive the sustained assault of modern heavy hitters without emblem and limit break support. His survivability relies heavily on his passives and family bonus rather than raw stat density.
Special Skill: The Call to Hibernate
This skill is a fascinating mix of crowd control and damage amplification.
This is the core. Enemies cannot attack or gain mana. This is effectively a "Silence" and "Mana Stop" rolled into one. However, the "wakes on damage" clause is the double-edged sword.
This is massive. If you time it right, the first hit that wakes an enemy up deals catastrophic damage. This synergizes incredibly well with heavy single-target snipers.
This was a buff added post-release (he originally did no damage). It's a "soft" hit, mainly there to chip away health or finish off low HP targets, but it ensures he isn't completely passive.
1200 HP over 4 turns is substantial (300 per turn). It provides great sustain for your team while the enemy is napping.
Minion and Fiend damage does NOT wake targets
. This is critical. You can have a minion-heavy team whittling down sleeping enemies without breaking the crowd control.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

Upon release, Teddy was viewed as a "niche" hero. The Sleep mechanic (first seen on Balbar) was considered clunky. However, his buffs (adding damage and increasing healing) made him a solid support option.
His stats are undeniably outdated. He lacks the raw bulk of 2025/2026 heroes.
He is not a "plug and play" hero for top 100 defenses. However, in

wars or tournaments, his Sleep can be devastating if the enemy doesn't have a cleanser ready. In PvE (Towers, Events), he allows you to control waves and charge your snipers safely. He remains a "fun" card rather than a "meta" card.

This review focuses on the base version of Teddy, as no costume is currently available for him. If a costume were to be released, one would hope it addresses his lower stat line and perhaps offers a version of Sleep that is less fragile to accidental damage.
Given his role is to survive long enough to cast Sleep and keep the team healed, you should prioritize his bulk.

One stray tile or slash attack wakes the enemy up.
Base stats are low compared to modern heroes.
While Minions don't wake enemies, almost everything else does, requiring careful board play.
At Very Fast speed, he can lock down enemies repeatedly.
Great for controlling boss waves. Sleep the bosses, charge your skills, and nuke them one by one.
His family bonus and passive rely on buffs, and his HoT counts as a buff.

Do not pair Teddy with DoT (Damage over Time) heroes like
if you want to keep enemies asleep. The tick damage will wake them up immediately at the start of their turn.

If you pulled Teddy early, he is a fantastic healer and controller. Don't worry about the complex strategies; just use him to pause the enemy while you heal up. He’s worth leveling for his healing alone in the early game.

Teddy is a luxury toy. If you have the spare mats and love "control" playstyles, he's fun. But don't prioritize him over modern meta heroes. He finds his best home in your 4th or 5th War team, specifically for countering Defense-Down heavy defenses.
"Balbar crawled so that this hero could run."
- The community immediately recognized him as a better version of Balbar, the original sleep hero.
"Imagine Freya/Teddy/Motega. Death by a thousand paper cuts."
- Players quickly identified the Minion synergy as his strongest use case.
"One stray tile is all it takes."
- The common frustration. Using Teddy requires you to stop matching tiles into empty space if you want the sleep to hold, which is counter-intuitive to generating mana.

Teddy is the definition of a "high skill floor" hero. In the hands of a thoughtful player who manages the board carefully, he can perform miracles. In the hands of an auto-play AI, he's a disaster who wakes everyone up immediately. He’s not going to break the game in 2026, but he brings a unique mechanic that keeps gameplay fresh.
for stats. Level him for the fun factor, not for the leaderboard.
